channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Rubio could face an unfriendly reception from close G7 allies over Trump's policies
item.description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio may be walking into unusually unfriendly territory this week when he meets his counterparts from the Group of 7 industrialized democracies.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Kuwait frees a group of jailed Americans, including contractors held on drug charges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Kuwait has released a group of American prisoners, including veterans and military contractors jailed for years on drug-related charges, in a move seen as a gesture of goodwill between two allies, a representative for the detainees told The Associated Press on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tariffs stoke anxiety with Minnesota officials over uncertain impacts
item.description: Minnesota U.S. Senator Amy Klobuchar said she’s concerned about the consequences the tariffs imposed by the Trump administration. She spoke at a press conference Wednesday that featured a Minnesota farmer who worries about the impacts on agriculture.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: New York assemblyman tries to confront Tom Homan over arrest of Columbia University anti-Israel activist
item.description: New York state Assembly member and New York City mayoral candidate Zohran Mamdani tried to confront border czar Tom Homan during his visit to Albany on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Education Department guts its civil rights office
item.description: The Education Department’s civil rights branch is losing nearly half its staff in the Trump administration’s layoffs.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Lone Democrat to back House GOP spending bill explains decision
item.description: Rep. Jared Golden (D-Maine) defied House Democratic leadership Tuesday when he voted for the GOP’s bill to avert a government shutdown — the only one in the caucus to support the stopgap. Golden’s “yes” vote was not the decisive one, since Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Transition period to curb government hiring driven inflation: Stephen Miller
item.description: White House Deputy Chief of Staff for Policy and Homeland Security Adviser Stephen Miller joins NewsNation to discuss the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: House GOP tees up final vote on bill to avert shutdown
item.description: House Republicans on Tuesday advanced their bill to avert an end-of-the-week government shutdown, teeing up a final vote on the legislation later in the day. The chamber voted 216-214 to adopt a rule — which governs debate on legislation — for Speaker Mike Johnson’s (R-La.) continuing resolution.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Republican-led effort to halt climate change lawsuits in Democratic-led states
item.description: The Supreme Court has rejected a lawsuit from Republican attorneys general in 19 states aimed at blocking climate change suits against the oil and gas industry from Democratic-led states, including Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Wall Street ends manic day modestly lower after briefly dropping more than 10% below its record
item.description: The U.S. stock market fell further Tuesday following President Donald Trump’s latest escalation in his trade war, briefly pulling Wall Street 10% below its record set last month. And like it's been most of the past few weeks, the market’s slide on Tuesday was erratic and dizzying.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Education Department says 60 universities under investigation for antisemitism
item.description: The Department of Education’s Office of Civil Rights (OCR) is investigating 60 universities for antisemitism, according to letters sent on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: US-Canada products are 99% tariff-free: Canadian ambassador to US
item.description: Canada's ambassador to the United States said 99% of products traded between the two nations are tariff-free.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Florida seeks easier voting access for hurricane-displaced citizens
item.description: Last year's hurricanes displaced thousands of Floridians, turning their lives upside down and making it even harder for some to vote during a crucial election year. Now, a Pinellas County state lawmaker wants to make it easier for people to cast their ballots after a storm.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Race for NYC mayor: Public safety and Cuomo defense money
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Candidates in the race for New York City mayor continue to take aim at former Gov. Andrew Cuomo's public safety agenda and tax dollars being spent on his legal troubles.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Florida bill would require some elementary school students to learn cursive
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — A new Florida bill would require some elementary school students to learn cursive writing. The bill, HB 921, would require all public schools to provide cursive writing instruction to students in grades 2 through 5.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Housing policies cited as major reason families forced to give up pets
item.description: Pet rescues and animal shelters hear it over and over: a family has to give up their dog because they had to move and the new place has a rule.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump threatens tariffs on Canadian dairy, lumber products as soon as Friday
item.description: Access to dairy and lumber markets have long been two of the biggest points of contention in the otherwise strong economic relationship between the U.S. and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Social Security offices in NY set to close amid DOGE budget cuts
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Several Social Security offices in New York will be closing as the Department of Government Efficiency continues to cut federal spending. The closures come after the Social Security Administration said it found over $800 million in savings for the 2025 fiscal year.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11
